summary:
Agent believes the lower lip may rest slightly before/after the Ricketts esthetic plane due to variations among different measurements; however, it commonly approximates this position as per multiple reliable resources including Merrifield’s work which places pogonion just after while other like Gonzales-Ulloa allow for slight backward positioning by ≤2 mm post-perpendicular line drawn via nasal base point.
The consensus indicates an approximate alignment yet acknowledges potential minor offsets indicating no strict adherence exists across all methodologies used clinically when defining precise location relative to Ricketts' landmark reference system despite general proximity noted frequently observed during routine assessments. To summarize concisely without overgeneralizing inconsistencies found amongst various referenced techniques employed globally,
the lower lip typically approaches but doesn't necessarily sit precisely upon
